summary: The R7 retrieval layer is approved for continued internal use, teacher-facing non-authoritative lookup, and lesson-authoring support with warnings and human review. It is not approved for student diagnostics, adaptive routing, student-facing AI, automatic lesson sequencing, automatic mastery decisions, summative assessment decisions, or unreviewed student-facing publication.
accepted_outcomes: Reference health reports may use retrieval outputs.
Teacher-facing non-authoritative lookup may use the retrieval layer when authority and warning labels are preserved.
Lesson-authoring support may use retrieval to find units, terms, evidence, related exercises, and warnings when human review remains required.
blocked_outcomes: Do not use retrieval for student diagnostics.
Do not use retrieval for adaptive routing.
Do not use retrieval for student-facing AI.
Do not use retrieval for automatic lesson sequencing.
Do not use retrieval for automatic mastery decisions.
Do not use retrieval for summative assessment decisions.
Do not use retrieval for unreviewed student-facing publication.

explicit_decisions.decision: Close R7.4 as pass_with_conditions, not pass.
Allow internal retrieval, teacher-facing non-authoritative lookup, and lesson-authoring support with warnings and human review.
Generated reports may appear only as diagnostics or warnings and must not be primary evidence or curriculum authority.
Pending-review and diagnostic-only edges may appear only when visibly marked and not displayed as approved knowledge.
Target exercises must be split into per-exercise chunks before production teacher-facing use.

conditions_to_reopen_or_pass: Retrieval output must preserve and display source authority.
Retrieval output must preserve and display edge status.
Generated-report results must be marked diagnostic_only and not primary evidence.
Pending-review edges must be visibly marked and not displayed as approved knowledge.
Diagnostic-only edges must be visibly marked as quality-control signals, not curriculum authority.
Evidence anchors must be surfaced where available.
Results without evidence anchors must not imply direct evidence.
Low-score lexical matches must show score or match-strength warnings.
Target-exercise retrieval must display the current large-chunk limitation.
Target exercises must be split into per-exercise chunks before production teacher-facing use.
Lesson-authoring support must remain human-reviewed and non-authoritative.
Student diagnostics, adaptive routing, and student-facing AI remain blocked.
